pattern: Processing arrival and departure events in time order while recycling freed chairs through a min-heap of available seat numbers.
hints: Sort the friends by arrival time, and remember which original index is your target.
Keep a pool of freed chair numbers; when a friend arrives, reuse the smallest freed chair, otherwise hand out a brand new one.
Process events chronologically: at each arrival, first release every chair whose occupant has already left, then assign the smallest available chair from a min-heap.

edge_inputs.description: target sits second on a fresh chair
target arrives last and gets a new chair
minimum of two friends, target is first arrival
a chair frees exactly at the moment another arrives and must be reused
target is the earliest arriver in a heavily overlapping set
leave equals next arrival so the chair is freed in time

stress.description: many friends with distinct arrivals and random spans, target somewhere in the middle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    arrivals = rng.sample(range(1, 100000), n)
    times = []
    for a in arrivals:
        leave = rng.randrange(a + 1, 100001)
        times.append([a, leave])
    target = rng.randrange(0, n)
    return [times, target]

solutions.python:
import heapq

class Solution:
    def smallestChair(self, times, targetFriend):
        target_arrival = times[targetFriend][0]
        events = sorted(range(len(times)), key=lambda i: times[i][0])
        free = list(range(len(times)))
        heapq.heapify(free)
        occupied = []
        for idx in events:
            arr, leave = times[idx]
            while occupied and occupied[0][0] <= arr:
                _, ch = heapq.heappop(occupied)
                heapq.heappush(free, ch)
            chair = heapq.heappop(free)
            if arr == target_arrival:
                return chair
            heapq.heappush(occupied, (leave, chair))
        return -1
